content:
9 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

created by geminiI gave the exact same product brief to ten different AI coding agents and told each one to build a working SaaS from scratch. Same spec, same database schema, same deadline of one weekend. What came back on Monday morning was not close. Some agents shipped something I could actually deploy. Others produced code that looked confident and fell apart the moment a real user touched it.
The moment that actually shocked me came from the agent I expected the least trouble from. More on that below. This is the full breakdown of what happened, what each agent actually got right, what it cost me, and which one I am still using six weeks later.
A note before you read on: I am keeping the ten repositories from this test. If you want to see the actual commit history, migrations, or test runs behind any of the claims here, ask in the comments and I will share the ones you are curious about.
Why I Ran This Experiment
Every week there is a new post claiming some agent is “the best AI coding tool of 2026.” I stopped trusting those rankings a long time ago because most of them are built on a single prompt, not a real product. A todo app does not tell you how an agent behaves on day three of a project, when the codebase has grown, the schema has changed…
